reviews.review_msg: Very engaging lectures and a very interesting final project. Make sure to choose a good team because you'll be working with them for the entire term.

Feyza is hands down one of the best instructors at SFU.  At first I thought it was strange how everything was "gamified".  BUT!  She was absolutely hilarious, engaging, dedicated and informative.  I absolutely loved her and the course!  Would recommend!!!

Feyza is extremely intelligent and she makes learning fun with her course design. Expectations for the course were set very early on, and she stayed consistent throughout the semester. I love how encouraging she was and the final project hackathon was a great way to test our knowledge.
She is a good instructor, given the clear instructions and amazing lectures for each week
Project was a lot of fun and she also gives you all the resources you need to succeed in the course (lecture recordings, practice problems + solutions, assignment solutions, etc.)
Actually can focus in here lectures. They're recorded as well. Project was very interesting and actually gave us a real world use. For well put together class.

Great prof, easy to follow examples that make course material easy to comprehend as all the lectures are recorded. One tip I would have that isn't mentioned in other reviews is that the midterm and final averages are high so make sure you don't fall behind on those as this is a curved course. Our midterm avg was a 87.
Exams were extremely fair other than that one random fill in the blank question that was incomprehensible. Grading is as clear as glass, is accessible outside of class. Truly a generational goat.

Devadrita is probably the best prof I have had at SFU! Although the concepts may initially seem difficult, her clear explanations and teaching pace make them much easier to understand; however, the midterm average is 87%. She is also incredibly smart and super nice!

I don't usually write these but Dr. Nair is easily one of the best professors I have had at SFU. You are doing yourself a disservice if you dont take BUS 373 with her. She had clear, well-paced lectures with lots of in-class time for examples and working through problems.

She is a very nice professor; I have taken her courses BUS 232 and BUS 373, and it was a super good experience learning and taking exams in her courses. Everything on the exams will be the content she taught in class, and she is very caring to us :)

Professor Negar's lectures are always straight to the point and clear. Her explanations during class are easy to follow through and she always welcomes student engagement and questions. She even helps with giving students hints on what's coming for the midterm and final exam. I would take more of her courses if I had the chance

I don't think she is suited to teach for this course; she is teaching 373 for the first time. I have taken 232 with her; she is so great and familiar with that one, but this one is not! Very short time to take a quiz with so much work is suck! Choose another prof.

Salar is a nice person but had a hard time explaining many of the basic concepts in operations management.  He spent a lot of time explaining concepts incorrectly and had to be corrected by his students on a handful of occasions.  The final exam related only marginally to the taught material.

Salar is a chill dude. He teaches and explains the concepts in the course very well. There were often awkward silences during the lectures but it's because he is a new prof. I'm sure he's going to be better after a few semesters. He posts all notes from class on canvas so if you miss a class it's easy to catch up. Overall great prof.

Gohram was a good lecturer in hindsight; however, he was unorganized, said conflicting things when it came to grading after he made a major mistake, and flat-out refused to take accountability when asked about it. I enjoyed the class and learned a lot, but he came off as difficult at the end.

Gohram is genuinely just a great dude and a great prof. Clearly cares a lot about student success and put a lot of work into the course. Grades are mostly determined by the midterm and group presentation so work hard on those. Overall class was pretty easy.
Best prof ever I haven taken at SFU Beedie! Never miss this A+ section everyone!

He gave me the first A+ in my BBA life (last term, with a 373)! I heard my friends say he is coming back and going to teach again, just come to write it to my friends! GO GO GO! If you did not choose his sections, you will regret it forever, so PLEASE CHOOSE HIM, Buddy!

Extraordinarily pleasant, he's a model for all Beedie faculty ~ he doesn't teach at SFU often, so if you see his name, don't hesitate to grab it! Quick! You have to be quick, in our classes he gave so many A's and B's above!

I have to compliment this professor! Took BUS336 & BUS373 with him, both were the “Easiest 100%” exams, and you didn't even have to travel to the classroom to learn well. Average didn't affect the ratio of A's - B+'s he gave! Best professor in life, bar none！！！！

He completed his course about a year ago for BUS 373, and the exam was very easy, similar to childhood calculations and super straightforward. It's easy to score full marks, and he gave more A's than other faculty, so please choose him without any doubts.
The assignments are very easy, provide free credits, and help you apply what you've learned in real-world situations. Office hours are extremely helpful — he is always patient and approachable.
I enjoy his teaching style, which offers very helpful instructions. Please work with him! Other 373 instructors are not good to study with, he is the GOAT!
Best instructor I have ever met at SFU, he is the best choice for this course!
I really like her teaching style; she is a smart, young, and caring instructor who really wants her students to succeed and get improved.
Been 10 days dince the final exam has been written, still have not recieved a grade, and for all the other classes for which the final was done the grade has been released.

The professor was caring; however, the course structure was unexpected, the professor didn't show up for the midterm nor the final. The midterm length was told to be 1.5 hours. However upon, arrival was 2.5 hours. The course concepts were presented a bit confusingly, and the final and midterm were lengthy but were fair overall.

Prof didn't show up for midterm AND final; Very simple concepts were presented in a very complex way (both in lectures and lecture notes); Excruciatingly long 16-20 page midterm and final; Couldn't answer students' questions during lectures; Professor seems caring but is not fit to teach. She seems confused about the course content herself.

she's not a bad prof tbh, the three hour lectures are a bit long sometimes and can be hard to follow. But she tries her best to explain material! you can tell she cares, exams are fair and based on lectures and assignments

Quite a dry lecture and class. We spend 30mins or more going over the same simple questions and it seems like she does not know the answers to questions students ask. Although I think she cares for the class and is a nice person, I cannot recommend this class.
Everything is super unorganized! Final Exam is cumulative, not like the other (They even provided the Formula Sheet).
It was extremely chaotic. She herself didn't even know what she wanted to teach.
OMG….to much to digest at once. It's like watching a movie. Non stop. Probably worse prof until now.

Possibly the worst professor I've ever had. Unorganized lectures. Spends the first 15 min of every class figuring out what she even wants to say. Glosses over important concepts and explains in such a way that makes the simplest concept the hardest thing you'v ever learned. I suggest you save your time and money and not take the course with her.
Goes too fast in class. Classes are not organized. would rather learn by myself at home. make simple concepts too complicated. Not at all recommended.
She is a nice prof, know how to teach well. Could say a good choice

Mona is probably one of the best profs in Beedie, especially for a course like 373. She taught exceptionally well and made sure the students understood the material before moving on. Lecture speed is not too slow or fast, and if you know the material, just go on your phone for a bit. Would recommend taking 373 with her when you can.

Honestly, Mona is one of the best professors I have had at SFU. She explains everything so well and is so kind and compassionate to students whenever they need help. She was always clear on expectations and provided a ton of helpful practice problems and detailed solutions. She deserves a more than a 5/5 and I highly recommend her to anyone in BUS.

the classroom is so hot in Room WMC3520 i almost die every lecture. There is a fan sitting next to her that she wont turn on while i'm dying from the heat. Great professor, but the heat is killing me. i legit wear shorts and t shirt in winter lectures and i'm still dying from the heat, its legit like sitting in an oven for 3 hours. Great prof 10/10
I think this course is easy interms of it's structure(no finals), but is useful
